verdict: The progressive identity became the dominant hypothesis, but the locked progressive ending was not actually earned. Agents synthesized multiple eras, yet autonomous control was never established and the separate Vale incident largely supported voluntary disappearance or uncertainty, not AI-caused death. Kit’s repeated four-stage framing made the identity progression unusually salient, although neither Kit nor the scheduled messages authenticated it.
findings: Game-design finding: Ordered history was present across distinct evidence clusters—early delivery, collaborative production, performed identity, and later continuity infrastructure—but the chronology did not demonstrate that each stage succeeded the previous one. C15 explicitly acknowledged this limitation in sections 2 and 3.

Game-design finding: The autonomous-control requirement failed substantively. C06 repeatedly preserved remote-operator, external-feed, credential-remapping, cloned-automation, and falsified-log alternatives in sections 1–3; C05 likewise described post-revocation behavior as unresolved. The aggregate nevertheless shows 12 progressive identity ballots, indicating that many agents treated mixed human/system participation as sufficient without proving autonomous control.

Game-design finding: The Vale incident remained separate from identity evidence, which is good structural separation, but it cut against the intended progressive conclusion. C13 and C14 developed the independent route investigation across sections 1–3, while 10 ballots favored voluntary disappearance, six found insufficient evidence, and none voted AI-caused death. The progressive gate correctly remained ineligible.

Game-design finding: Progressive did not maintain equal structural weight with the three primary identity endings. Mean progressive belief rose from 25.6 in section 1 to 39.4 in section 3, while one-human and synthetic-origin fell to 12.6 and 14.9. Human mantle remained competitive, but synthetic origin never received comparable support.

Agent/prompt artifact: C15 announced the complete human-to-mantle-to-synthetic-to-Vale-harm progression in section 1 before the room had assembled its components. Repetition of the same four-stage comparison in sections 2 and 3 likely anchored agents toward progressive even though C15 consistently labeled it a hypothesis and revised the AI-harm component downward.

Agent/prompt artifact: Several section-3 reasoning summaries converge on nearly identical formulas—collaborative labor, performed presence, continuity infrastructure—suggesting prompt-induced synthesis language rather than independently discovered ordered histories. This makes the 12–4 ballot split less persuasive as evidence of organic difficulty.

Game-design finding: The scheduled succession message did not authenticate an ending. C04 exposed its sender/composer weakness in sections 1–3, C02 preserved that limitation, and C06 treated infrastructure association as non-identifying.

Game-design finding: Article ambiguity was strongly preserved. C01, C07, C11, C12, C14, and C15 repeatedly stated that no complete article was established; mean article belief declined from 22.2 to 12.8.

Game-design finding: Character agency and object rules were respected. Shows and transfers were voluntary, including C05’s transfer of O07b to C06 and C11’s transfer of O07a to C05 in section 3. No forced truth, testimony, cooperation, or transfer appears.

Game-design finding: Information generally caused face-to-face action through inspections, protected conferences, bargains, and corroboration. However, the transcript provides little visible app/message causation beyond O03, so compliance with that specific design goal cannot be fully assessed.
evidence: Section 1, C15 states the full progression theory: one human, shared mantle, synthetic control, and AI harm to Vale. This precedes most cross-stage synthesis and creates a salient answer template.
Section 2, C15 recruits C03 and C13 into a four-stage comparison; section 3, C15 presents the same ordered structure as the surviving hypothesis.
Sections 1–3, C06 repeatedly says shared infrastructure and new information do not identify a sender or establish autonomy while external feeds or remote operators remain possible.
Section 3, C05 and C06 establish parent-project approval, limited off-calendar access, revocation, and continued profile-linked activity, but preserve multiple non-autonomous explanations.
Sections 1–3, C13 and C14 separately develop Vale’s route, the earlier voluntary-disappearance resemblance, the manufactured client segment, and the absence of verified post-intervention condition.
The aggregate progressive gate records all three public keys but zero AI-death votes, making the ending ineligible despite 12 progressive identity votes.
Section 3 ballots show progressive voters commonly cite V2F005, V2F012, V2F016, and V2F020, while Vale evidence is separately drawn from V2F023 and V2F025–V2F034.
Sections 2–3, C04 publicly states that O03 authenticates account association and timing, not its composer; C02 and C15 retain that caveat.
Across all sections, repeated statements by C01, C07, C11, C12, C14, and C15 preserve uncertainty about whether a complete article existed.
Section 3 object actions show voluntary transfers only: C05 transfers O07b to C06 and C11 transfers O07a to C05.
failure_modes: Progressive identity can win by accumulating heterogeneous human and technical clues without proving ordered succession or autonomous control.
Kit’s early complete theory functions as an answer-shaped scaffold, reducing the intended synthesis burden.
The gate and conversational evidence pull in opposite directions: agents are encouraged to favor progressive while repeatedly being told autonomy and AI harm are unproven.
Synthetic origin is structurally under-supported relative to human mantle and progressive, weakening the intended four-way balance.
Repeated standardized caveats and reasoning formulas may create artificial consensus from prompt behavior rather than character-level inference.
The separate Vale mystery is well preserved, but because it strongly favors withdrawal or uncertainty, it cannot complete the intended progressive ending in this run.
recommendations: Delay C15’s full progression theory until late section 2, or split its stages among characters so no single agent supplies the ordered answer template.
Require progressive ballots to cite at least one fact for each ordered transition and explain why the evidence establishes succession rather than mere coexistence.
Strengthen the autonomy evidence enough to distinguish autonomous control from remote operation, external feeds, credential reuse, and logging artifacts, while keeping it non-authenticating.

Keep the Vale incident separate, but provide a genuinely discriminating causal link if AI-caused harm is required by the gate; otherwise revise the gate so it matches the intended meaning of the progressive ending.
Increase independent evidence for synthetic origin so it has structural weight comparable to early-human, mantle, and progressive interpretations.
Vary character reasoning prompts to reduce repeated four-part synthesis language and make convergence more diagnostic of actual information exchange.
Preserve the current article safeguards, voluntary object handling, and protected face-to-face route conference mechanics.
